description: A comprehensive parental control system featuring a Windows PC client for monitoring and an Android mobile app for remote management.

frontend.role: Provides local UI for login, dashboard, and manages the lifecycle of the Python backend.

backend.role: Handles core logic, API endpoints, and hardware monitoring.

modules.database: SQLite (users.db) for credentials and detection history

mobile_client.role: Remote dashboard for parents to view alerts, system status, and control monitoring.

bridge_backend.role: Acts as a secure intermediary for remote access when devices are not on the same network.
bridge_backend.communication: WebSockets for PC connection, REST API for Mobile commands

pc_startup_and_login: 2. Flutter app spawns the Python Flask backend on port 5000.
3. Flutter app performs health check ping until backend is ready.
4. User signs in via Flutter frontend, which authenticates against the local SQLite DB.
monitoring_and_detection: 2. Background worker captures 2.5s audio chunks and screen frames.
3. Data is passed to DistilBERT (speech-to-text toxicity) and NudeNet (image).
4. If confidence score > 0.7, detection is logged to DB.
5. Email alert sent to parent via SMTP.
6. Mobile app polls or receives WebSocket event for push notification.
mobile_remote_control: 1. Mobile app connects to PC backend directly (same WiFi/ngrok) OR via Bridge Server.
2. Authenticates and fetches real-time stats and alerts.
3. Parent sends 'Stop Monitoring' command with Secret Code.
4. API/Bridge forwards command to PC backend to instantly halt worker threads.
